# UC-22.15.42 · High-Rate Modbus Exception Responses (Potential DoS)

## Description

Detects exception storms targeting PLCs that may indicate denial-of-service attacks against control I/O.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_protocol and sourcetypes edge_hub_modbus. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Rate-limit at gateway; (2) ACL offending IP; (3) correlate with IDS; (4) verify PLC CPU load via vendor API. Indexes required: `index=ot_protocol`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=edge_hub_modbus`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `edge_hub_modbus` with `exception_code`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Rate-limit at gateway; (2) ACL offending IP; (3) correlate with IDS; (4) verify PLC CPU load via vendor API.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" isnotnull(exception_code) earliest=-4h
| bin _time span=1m
| stats count by _time, dest_plc, src_ip, exception_code
| where count>500
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**High-Rate Modbus Exception Responses (Potential DoS) (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 6.1)** — Detects exception storms targeting PLCs that may indicate denial-of-service attacks against control I/O.

Documented **Data sources**: `edge_hub_modbus` with `exception_code`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: edge_hub_modbus. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, dest_plc, src_ip, exception_code** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>500` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value (peaks/min).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" isnotnull(exception_code) earliest=-4h
| bin _time span=1m
| stats count by _time, dest_plc, src_ip, exception_code
| where count>500
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Table, Single value (peaks/min). Key fields: `dest_plc`, `exception_code`, `src_ip`.
